scope.summary: Local persistence foundation (ADR-0005): a `store` layer backed by bundled SQLite (rusqlite) plus an encrypted credential store. A random 32-byte master key is held in the OS keychain (keyring); the credential triple {imei, cookie, user_agent} is AES-256-GCM encrypted with that key and stored in SQLite keyed by account_id. On startup, saved accounts are re-logged-in/listened automatically (session restore), fixing the 'restart -> back to QR' UX. Credentials are never written in plaintext and never cross IPC.
goals: store/: rusqlite (bundled) connection + schema/migrations (accounts, credentials); typed repository functions — no raw SQL above store
store/: keyring-backed master key (generate-once, load) + AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt of the credential blob; rusqlite/keyring/aes-gcm types never leak above store

command/: after QR/cookie login, persist account + encrypted credential; on startup, load saved accounts, decrypt, and re-login/listen each (reuse login_and_listen); expired credential flips account state to reauth-needed without disturbing other accounts
Credentials are AES-GCM encrypted at rest; only the master key lives in the keychain; no plaintext secret on disk and no token values across IPC
nonGoals: Multi-account SessionManager refactor (session-manager) — reuse the current in-process store path
Backfilling history that predates first login (no upstream API)

acceptance.description: store/ creates the SQLite schema and round-trips an encrypted credential: save -> load decrypts to the same triple; a wrong/absent master key fails to decrypt. rusqlite/keyring/aes-gcm confined to store.
command/ persists the account + encrypted credential after login and exposes a startup restore path; compiles and keeps token values out of IPC DTOs.

Live/manual: after QR login the credential round-trips via keychain+SQLite; relaunch auto-restores the account (no QR). Evidence captures only non-secret diagnostics; the SQLite credentials row is verified to be ciphertext (no plaintext cookie/imei).
